channel.title: JLT Spotlight (Facebook)
channel.description: Weekly Cruise, Hotel & Destination of the Week from JLT Group

item.title: One for the cruise specialists in here… 🛳️
item.description:
One for the cruise specialists in here… 🛳️
A Regent Seven Seas specialist called Harriet sailed Seven Seas Splendor Lisbon to Monte Carlo with her mother, seven nights, and wrote it up port by port. Not a brochure pass-on. Her own trip, her own words, including a sunset steak at Prime 7 she described as the best she has ever eaten, and dolphins spotted from the balcony off Tangier that her mother had waited a lifetime to see.
The detail that keeps coming up in her report is space. No queuing, no scramble for a lounger, always a pool spot and a table when you want one. That is the conversation to have with the experienced cruiser who is on the fence about trading up. ✨
Two Splendor sailings are bookable now: a 14-night Med in October 2027 (Lisbon to Rome) and an 11-night Canada and New England foliage sailing in September 2026. Match one to a client on your list this week.
Full spotlight on the website… link in the comments 💬

item.title: Worth knowing before 30 July. ⏳
item.description:
Worth knowing before 30 July. ⏳
The Exsus offer on Viceroy at Ombria runs out then: seven nights from £1,095pp with a complimentary night included and £100pp saved.
🌿 The property itself is worth knowing regardless. Not a beach hotel. Sits inland near Querenca, built to resemble a traditional Portuguese hill village on a 150-hectare estate.
Suites run to 173 sqm with kitchens or kitchenettes. There is an award-winning golf course, a dedicated spa and a dark-sky stargazing programme in a certified reserve.
This is the answer for the client who says Algarve but means somewhere quieter and more characterful.
Full spotlight on the website… link in the comments 💬

item.title: The client for this one is that well-travelled person who has done Beijing, Shanghai and Xi'an and comes back to you saying, "I want something different." 🌍
item.description:
The client for this one is that well-travelled person who has done Beijing, Shanghai and Xi'an and comes back to you saying, "I want something different." 🌍
Chongqing is the answer. Light rail running straight through a high-rise building, drone shows over the skyline at night, and underneath it all, a genuine hot-spring heritage tied to traditional Chinese medicine. Wendy Wu Tours has built a retreat around it: eight days from £3,990pp, with a qigong master, reflexology and a Miao Medicine Clinic visit on the itinerary… some of them exclusive to the operator.
The catch is there is only one departure: 9 May 2027. Early conversation is the one that secures the seat. 🗓
Full spotlight on the website… link in the comments 💬

item.title: The client for this one is the family who has done Florida twice and is ready for a US Southwest change of pace. 🌵
item.description:
The client for this one is the family who has done Florida twice and is ready for a US Southwest change of pace. 🌵
Arizona checks more boxes than most agents realise. Tucson's guest ranches give multi-gen families riding, hiking and Saguaro National Park scenery, while Scottsdale handles the luxury end with serious spa and resort credentials. One state, three or four distinct experiences, and a daily BA service into Phoenix.
Visit Arizona has a live agent webinar this Tuesday 7 July at 11am BST, with a £50 shopping voucher prize draw on the call. Worth blocking an hour while the autumn booking window is open.
Full spotlight on the website… link in the comments 💬

item.title: The client for this one is clear the moment you read it… HNW, sporting, multi-generational, or anyone circling a September or October Scottish escape. 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿
item.description:
The client for this one is clear the moment you read it… HNW, sporting, multi-generational, or anyone circling a September or October Scottish escape. 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿
Gleneagles has a senior ghillie who designed and hand-ties the estate's own salmon fly, the Gleneagles Glory, drawing its colours from the hotel's interior. Every guest on a fishing trip leaves with one. That kind of depth is not something a newer property can manufacture, and it is exactly what your client is paying for.
Three championship golf courses, a Michelin two-star restaurant (the only one in Scotland at publication), a gundog school, a riding school… the whole party can be busy on the same estate, on the same stay. ✨
The autumn calendar still has room. Full spotlight on the website… link in the comments 💬

item.title: One for the expedition specialists in the group 🧭
item.description:
One for the expedition specialists in the group 🧭
Aurora Expeditions has turned 35 and they're marking it with a campaign built to move enquiries this week. The campaign runs to the end of September and pairs up to 30% off Antarctica 26/27 and Arctic 27 voyages with up to £2,600 air credit applied as an additional voyage discount. That long-haul flight cost to Ushuaia is usually the thing that makes a considering-it client hesitate. This takes it off the table.
The ships are the Greg Mortimer and Sylvia Earle. Small expedition vessels with X-Bow hulls 🌊 that cut through the Drake Passage rather than ride over it. Around 130 to 160 guests. Real Zodiac time, ice camping, citizen science tie-ins. This is not a mainstream brand with a polar add-on.
Pull your expedition enquiry list this week and match names to voyages before September closes.
Full spotlight on the website… link in the comments 💬

item.title: One for the long-haul winter-sun specialists… TUI has announced the only nonstop UK flights to Zanzibar, departing from Gatwick from 3 November 2027. Twice weekly, Wednesdays and Sundays, through to 22 March 2028. 🌊
item.description: One for the long-haul winter-sun specialists… TUI has announced the only nonstop UK flights to Zanzibar, departing from Gatwick from 3 November 2027. Twice weekly, Wednesdays and Sundays, through to 22 March 2028. 🌊 The connection has been the sticking point for years. That is now gone. Direct from Gatwick changes the conversation entirely, especially for clients who have been curious about the Spice Island but baulked at the journey. Lead-in prices start from £1,615 per person (two adults, all-inclusive, seven nights including flights and transfers). The Winter 2027 programme goes on sale 2 July 2026. Worth having your shortlist ready before that date. ✈️ Full spotlight on the website… link in the comments 💬

item.title: One for the back pocket, this week's Hotel Spotlight… 🏨
item.description: One for the back pocket, this week's Hotel Spotlight… 🏨 Bodrum Loft is the hotel for the client who wants Bodrum's glamour without the noise. Picture a little white village of minimalist villas stepping down a pine hillside to a private Aegean cove, barefoot beach-club mood, and a sea view from nearly every room. It is the calmer side of the peninsula… thirtysomething couples, Istanbul families, a sleepy bay, and a genuinely good table with Paper Moon right over the water. Villa kitchens make it a strong one for longer stays and honeymoons too. ✈️ Full spotlight on the website… link in the comments 💬

item.title: One for the luxury specialists in our community… 🚢
item.description: One for the luxury specialists in our community… 🚢 Silversea has opened a savings window worth knowing about this week. Up to 45 per cent off select voyages across all seven continents, and it closes on 30 June. That hard date is the point. It gives you a clear, premium reason to pick up the phone to your established luxury clients this week rather than waiting for them to come to you. A few autumn 2026 sailings worth flagging: Greek Isles and Istanbul from £3,550 pp on Silver Ray, and two overlapping Greek Isles departures on Silver Nova and Silver Spirit from £3,750–£3,850 pp. Good comparison material if a client is weighing itinerary over ship. ✈️ Full spotlight on the website… link in the comments 💬
